Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Discovery is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted in part and denied in part as to Document Request No. 1. The response is properly limited to personnel action forms identifying the individuals who were hired to fill positions. The response is not properly limited to positions for which Plaintiff applied. Objections to the scope of time requested are overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond as to the positions enumerated in the request.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory 1 for the positions enumerated in the request. Objections to the scope of time requested are overruled.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 207.7. The response is not properly limited to positions for which plaintiff applied. Defendant is ordered to respond for the categories enumerated in the interrogatory. The response is not properly limited to accommodations offered to or considered for Plaintiff. The reasonable limitations to accommodations are facts to be determined by the finder of fact.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 204.5. The response is inadequate as it does not describe the accommodations that were needed.
The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory 204.6. The response is adequate as it states that it will produce the documents that support the response.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 204.7. Reference to other interrogatories is inadequate.
The motion is granted as to the form of the verification. While the verification may be on information and belief as interrogatory responses are not required to be or allowed to be limited to personal knowledge. However, paragraph 3 is improper and is ordered stricken.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REP
